title: cerastium arvense var. oblongifolium
intro: Cerastium arvense var. oblongifolium, commonly known as field chickweed, is a perennial herb belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of field chickweed include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and treat digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Field chickweed can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including wounds, eczema, and menstrual cramps.
